channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Garlin Gilchrist announces Democratic bid to become Michigan's first Black governor
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Garlin Gilchrist II, a Democrat from Detroit who is Michigan's first Black lieutenant governor, announced he's jumping into the crowded 2026 race for governor on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Court asked to intervene after email tells USAID workers to destroy classified documents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A union for U.S. Agency for International Development contractors asked a federal judge Tuesday to intervene in any destruction of classified documents after an email ordered staffers to help burn and shred agency records.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: DHS ending collective bargaining for transportation security officers
item.description: "They gave as a justification a completely fabricated claim about union officials," AFGE National President Everett Kelley said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: TROY, Mich. (AP) — Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility, Michigan’s attorney general said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia protester arrested by ICE and facing deportation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Palestinian activist who helped lead student protests at Columbia University faces deportation after being arrested over the weekend by federal immigration officials.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: NTSB urges ban on some helicopter flights at Reagan airport after deadly collision
item.description: Federal investigators looking for the cause of the January collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people recommended a ban on some helicopter flights Tuesday to improve safety.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US bans flights to Haiti's capital until Sept. 8 as UN expert says gang violence is more dire
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Tuesday extended its ban on flights to Haiti’s capital until Sept. 8 because of escalating gang violence, which the U.N.’s human rights expert on the Caribbean nation said is more dire than ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: At trial's start, prosecutor blames Iran for plot to assassinate outspoken dissident
item.description: A prosecutor has told jurors that two defendants were hired guns for the government of Iran in a plot to assassinate an Iran-born journalist at her Brooklyn residence three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: National Park Service faces 30% payroll reduction
item.description: The reduction would not necessarily lead to a 30% cut in staffing, since some employees make more than others.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: NTSB makes 'urgent safety recommendations' after DC plane crash
item.description: NTSB investigators said there have been 15,214 "near-miss events" at DCA from Oct. 2021 to Dec. 2024.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Dad leads team to state tourney after coach's death
item.description: "I really think that we've helped him through that and he's helped us through this," said Rachel Cordell.
item.dc:creator: Adria Goins/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Medical helicopter crash in Mississippi kills 3
item.description: MADISON COUNTY, Miss. (AP) — A medical transport helicopter crashed in Mississippi on Monday, killing a pilot and two hospital workers on board, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declares mistrial in case against California judge accused of killing his wife
item.description: A judge has declared a mistrial after a jury came back with a split decision in the case against an Orange County judge accused of shooting and killing his wife following an argument in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities, including Northwestern University, under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles County said Monday that he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez because the brothers have repeatedly lied about why they killed their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alleged kidnapping attempt thwarted after victim slipped witness an SOS note at Utah gas station
item.description: A man is in custody after he allegedly attempted to kidnap a woman, only to have his attempt thwarted by the victim who slipped a note to a witness at a southern Utah gas station.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Fishermen pull 'wrapped' body out of water off coast of Massachusetts
item.description: The crew of the boat had reported finding the body in its fishing nets about 40 miles from the Massachusetts coast on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Stroller accessory recalled after reports of children getting hurt
item.description: More than 33,000 stroller fans have been recalled after their Louisiana-based importer received several reports of the fan blades injuring children. The recall, which concerns Nuby Stroller Fans, was initiated last week.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court takes up Colorado conversion therapy case
item.description: The Supreme Court granted a Colorado therapist’s request to review her challenge to a state law banning mental health care providers from engaging minors in conversion therapy, a discredited practice that attempts to change a person’s sexual orientation or gender identity to align with heterosexual
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump defends tariff policy
item.description: President Donald Trump continues to defend tariffs as a way to address unfair trade and illegal drugs coming across the border, but critics warn they'll cause higher prices for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: AI made its way to vineyards. Here's how the technology is helping make your wine
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When artificial intelligence-backed tractors became available to vineyards, Tom Gamble wanted to be an early adopter. He knew there would be a learning curve, but Gamble decided the technology was worth figuring out. The third-generation farmer bought one autonomous tractor.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: An early version of the Egg McMuffin included a surprising condiment
item.description: For a brief time, McDonald's envisioned their now-signature breakfast sandwich with a sweet component.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Courts grapple with 'King' Trump
item.description: The White House sees few, if any, limits on President Trump's executive powers in his second term, but the federal court system is much less sure.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York governor declares state of emergency for wind-driven brush fire on Long Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fast-moving brush fires fanned by high winds burned through a large swath of land on New York's Long Island on Saturday, spewing thick gray smoke into the sky and prompting the evacuation of a military base and the closure of a major highway. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street Journal: 'Someone should sue' Trump over tariffs
item.description: In a recent op-ed, the board warned of a possible jolt in consumer prices once the tariffs go into full effect and urged someone to file a lawsuit in response.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4
